source: IHB — Institute of Human Biology, Basel (Roche) — Organoid-on-a-Chip Historical Database
note: All concordance rates are vs. GLP in-vivo preclinical studies (n≥3 species). BioNeMo concordance is vs. NVIDIA BioNeMo simulation predictions.

key_findings: ESR1 Y537S/D538G mutant organoids show 3.2× greater sensitivity to complete degraders vs. partial antagonists.
Combination with CDK4/6i (palbociclib) shows synergistic growth inhibition in 78% of ER+ organoid lines.
Intrinsic resistance mechanism identified in PIK3CA co-mutant lines — combination with PI3Ki required.
Dual GLP-1R/GIPR agonism shows 2.1× greater insulin secretion vs. GLP-1R single agonism in beta-cell organoids.
Gastric emptying delay validated in enteric nervous system organoids — correlates with weight loss signal.
Hepatic lipid reduction confirmed in liver-on-chip co-culture with adipose organoids.
KRAS G12C inhibitor resistance develops in 42% of organoid lines at 8 weeks — primarily via KRAS amplification or RAS bypass.
Combination with SHP2 inhibitor delays resistance onset by 3-4 weeks in organoid models.
STK11/KEAP1 co-mutations predict intrinsic resistance in 85% of tested lines.
PIK3CA H1047R-mutant organoids most sensitive; E545K/E542K moderate; C420R low sensitivity.
Hyperglycaemia feedback loop confirmed — mTOR re-activation seen in 60% of lines without insulin sensitiser.
Combination with fulvestrant (ERD) shows synergistic effect in ER+/PIK3CA-mut organoids.
Anti-TIGIT activity is CD8 T-cell dependent — organoids depleted of CD8+ cells show no response.
Combination with anti-PD-L1 shows synergistic T-cell activation in 68% of TIGIT-high/PD-L1-high lines.
PD-L1 TPS <1% lines show minimal benefit from anti-TIGIT monotherapy.
C481S BTK mutation causes 18× reduction in binding affinity for covalent BTKi — non-covalent maintains activity.
B-cell proliferation suppression confirmed in MS-relevant B-cell organoids.
Skin toxicity mechanism validated: BTK inhibition in keratinocyte co-cultures causes IL-17 pathway dysregulation.
Complete C5 inhibition achieved at predicted Cmin for recycling antibody design.
pH-dependent dissociation mechanism validated in lysosomal pH conditions — FcRn recycling confirmed.
No hook effect observed at supraphysiological C5 concentrations (key PNH risk).
Brain shuttle mechanism (TfR1 binding) confirms 8× increased CNS exposure vs. standard IgG.
Amyloid plaque clearance rate 92% in 28-week cerebral organoid model.
ARIA-E mechanism validated: perivascular oedema in 35% of apoE4-positive organoid lines.
95-98% hepatic AGT knockdown achieved at therapeutic doses in liver organoid model.
Duration of effect 6+ months per single dose in primary hepatocyte organoids.
GalNAc-ASGR1 uptake mechanism fully validated — no off-target silencing in non-hepatic organoids.
Protofilament-targeting antibody reduces Lewy body load by 65% in dopaminergic neuron organoids.
CNS penetration remains the key uncertainty — 0.2% CSF/serum ratio predicted.
Neuroinflammation biomarker (GFAP, NfL) reduction confirmed in iPSC-derived midbrain model.
TL1A blockade reduces fibroblast activation by 58% in TNFSF15-high colon organoids.
Anti-fibrotic benefit distinct from anti-inflammatory benefit — may be additive.
Response predicted in TNFSF15-high but not TNFSF15-low patients — biomarker selection critical.
IL-33 blockade reduces eosinophil recruitment by 72% in airway organoid model.
Benefit concentrated in type-2-low/eosinophil-low asthma subtype — distinct from mepolizumab/dupilumab.
ILC2 suppression mechanism confirmed — may benefit severe, refractory asthma not responding to IL-5/IL-4 axis.
Ligand-trap (anti-IL-6) provides faster suppression kinetics than receptor blockade in acute attack model.
AQP4-IgG-driven astrocyte damage reduced 81% in co-culture neurospheroid model.
Blood-brain barrier stabilisation effect confirmed — may reduce attack severity in NMOSD.
CD20 × CD3 bispecific T-cell engager drives complete B-cell depletion in 48h in organoid model.
CRS risk modelled by cytokine release assay — step-up dosing regimen validated.
Tumour microenvironment CD8/CD4 ratio predicts response (CD8/CD4 > 1.5 → high responder).
FcRH5 expression 3-4× higher in myeloma plasma cells vs. normal B-cells — high therapeutic window.
T-cell redirected killing confirmed in bone marrow organoid model.
BCMA escape mutants retain FcRH5 expression — useful for post-anti-BCMA salvage therapy.
Continuous intravitreal VEGF-A suppression confirms 6-month sustained drug release from port delivery system.
Retinal neovascularisation fully suppressed at therapeutic concentrations.
Implant biocompatibility validated in retinal organoid co-culture — no cytotoxicity signals.
FVIII expression 3-8× normal level achieved at therapeutic LNP doses in primary hepatocyte organoids.
Duration of expression 10-14 days per dose — consistent with predicted half-life.
Innate immune activation (IFN-I pathway) manageable with LNP formulation optimisation.
IL-6R blockade reduces astrocyte activation 75% in AQP4-IgG-positive NMOSD model.
pH-dependent recycling mechanism validated — supports Q4W subcutaneous dosing.
MOGAD co-morbidity risk: non-AQP4 NMOSD variants show lower response (42% vs. 78% in AQP4+ lines).
